steps.name: Tauri project scaffold and GitHub Actions workflow
steps.description: Initialize Tauri 2.0 project with Rust backend + React/TypeScript frontend. Create GitHub Actions workflow that builds Windows x64 binary on version tag push using tauri-apps/tauri-action and attaches artifact to GitHub Release.
criteria: Pushing a version tag triggers the build workflow
Successful build produces a GitHub Release with Windows x64 binary
Build failure prevents release publication

steps.name: npm package with postinstall binary download
steps.description: Create npm package.json with postinstall script that detects platform (win32-x64), downloads the correct binary from GitHub Release, and extracts to ~/.norbert/bin/. Register norbert-cc as bin entry.
criteria: npx github:pmvanev/norbert-cc downloads and installs the binary
norbert-cc command is available in the user's terminal after install
Network failure during download shows clear error with no partial files

steps.name: Binary size validation
steps.description: Add CI step verifying the packaged binary stays under 15MB. Fail the workflow if exceeded.
criteria: Pipeline asserts binary package is under 15 megabytes
Oversized binary fails the workflow before release publication

steps.name: System tray icon with tooltip
steps.description: Register Norbert icon in Windows 11 system tray on app launch. Tooltip displays app name and version sourced from shared constant.
criteria: Tray icon appears when norbert-cc is launched
Tooltip shows 'Norbert v0.1.0'
Tray icon persists across window open/close cycles

steps.name: Main window with status display
steps.description: Create React status window showing app name, version, status indicator, port, session count, and event count. Data fetched via Tauri IPC commands.
criteria: Window displays 'NORBERT v0.1.0', 'Status: Listening', 'Port: 3748'
Session and event counts show '0' on fresh launch
Version and port values sourced from shared constants

steps.name: Tray icon click toggles window
steps.description: Clicking tray icon opens main window if closed, closes if open. Closing window via close button hides window without terminating the application.
criteria: Tray click toggles window visibility
Window close button hides window; app continues running
Tray icon remains visible after window close

steps.name: Empty state display
steps.description: When no sessions exist, show 'Waiting for first Claude Code session...' message. Interface appears intentional, not broken.
criteria: Empty state message visible when session count is zero
Message disappears once first session data arrives
Interface looks intentional, not error-like

steps.name: Core domain types and port traits
steps.description: Define domain types (HookEvent, Session, EventType, AppStatus) in core module. Define port traits (EventStore, SettingsManager) in ports module. No adapter implementations yet.
criteria: Core types represent all walking skeleton domain concepts
Port traits define storage and settings abstractions
Core module has zero external dependencies

steps.name: SQLite adapter with schema initialization
steps.description: Implement EventStore port via SQLite. Create database at ~/.norbert/norbert.db with WAL mode, NORMAL synchronous. Initialize sessions and events tables with indexes.
criteria: Database created in data directory on first launch
journal_mode is WAL and synchronous is NORMAL
Sessions and events tables exist with correct schema

steps.name: Settings merge adapter
steps.description: Implement SettingsManager port. Read ~/.claude/settings.json, create byte-identical backup, surgically merge hook entries for 6 event types. Handle missing file and malformed JSON edge cases. Show restart notification on success.
criteria: Backup is byte-identical to original before any modification
Merged config retains all existing user settings and adds 6 hook entries
Malformed JSON skips merge with warning; no backup created
Missing file creates new config with hooks only; no backup
Merge is idempotent -- no duplicates on re-run

steps.name: HTTP hook receiver sidecar
steps.description: Build norbert-hook-server as Tauri sidecar binary. HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:3748 with POST /hooks/{event_type} route. Validate event type, persist to SQLite via EventStore, return 200 after write.
criteria: POST /hooks/{valid_type} with valid payload returns HTTP 200
Event persisted to database before acknowledgment sent
Unknown event type rejected with appropriate error status
Port-in-use shows clear error in UI

steps.name: Sidecar lifecycle and app composition
steps.description: Wire app startup: init DB, merge settings, spawn hook-receiver sidecar via Tauri sidecar API. Sidecar survives window close. Register IPC commands for frontend queries.
criteria: Sidecar starts on app launch and continues after window close
IPC commands return status, session count, and event count
Hook receiver port matches settings merge URLs

steps.name: Session capture and display
steps.description: SessionStart event creates session record; Stop event finalizes with duration. UI displays session count, event count, last session timestamp, and duration via IPC queries.
criteria: First captured session replaces empty state with session details
Session shows correct start timestamp and calculated duration
Multiple sessions accumulate with accurate total counts

steps.name: Live UI updates and status transitions
steps.description: Backend emits Tauri events on new hook data. Frontend updates counts within 1 second. Status transitions between 'Listening' and 'Active session'. Tray icon and tooltip reflect active state. Restart banner dismisses on first event.
criteria: Event count increments within 1 second of hook receipt
Status transitions between Listening and Active session
Tray icon and tooltip reflect active/idle state
Restart banner auto-dismisses on first event arrival

steps.name: Data durability and cross-component integration
steps.description: Verify pre-restart events survive app restart. Confirm database shared between sidecar and main window. Validate event type consistency across settings and receiver routes.
criteria: Events persisted before restart are visible after relaunch
Events stored by sidecar are readable by main window within 1 second
Event types in settings config match receiver route list exactly
